speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: It is too late. The life of all his blood

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Is touched corruptibly, and his pure brain,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Which some suppose the soul’s frail dwelling-house,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Doth, by the idle comments that it makes,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Foretell the ending of mortality.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: His Highness yet doth speak, and holds belief

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: That being brought into the open air

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: It would allay the burning quality

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: Of that fell poison which assaileth him.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Let him be brought into the orchard here.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Doth he still rage?

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: He is more patient

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: Than when you left him. Even now he sung.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: O vanity of sickness! Fierce extremes

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: In their continuance will not feel themselves.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Death, having preyed upon the outward parts,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Leaves them invisible, and his siege is now

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Against the mind, the which he pricks and wounds

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: With many legions of strange fantasies,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Which in their throng and press to that last hold

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Confound themselves. ’Tis strange that Death should sing.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: I am the cygnet to this pale faint swan,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Who chants a doleful hymn to his own death,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: And from the organ-pipe of frailty sings

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: His soul and body to their lasting rest.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Be of good comfort, prince, for you are born

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To set a form upon that indigest

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Which he hath left so shapeless and so rude.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Ay, marry, now my soul hath elbow-room.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: It would not out at windows nor at doors.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: There is so hot a summer in my bosom

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: That all my bowels crumble up to dust.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: I am a scribbled form drawn with a pen

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Upon a parchment, and against this fire

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Do I shrink up.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: How fares your Majesty?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Poisoned—ill fare—dead, forsook, cast off,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And none of you will bid the winter come

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To thrust his icy fingers in my maw,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Nor let my kingdom’s rivers take their course

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Through my burned bosom, nor entreat the North

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To make his bleak winds kiss my parchèd lips

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And comfort me with cold. I do not ask you much.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: I beg cold comfort, and you are so strait

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And so ingrateful, you deny me that.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: O, that there were some virtue in my tears

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: That might relieve you!

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The salt in them is hot.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Within me is a hell, and there the poison

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Is, as a fiend, confined to tyrannize

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: On unreprievable, condemnèd blood.

speaker: BASTARD
text: O, I am scalded with my violent motion

speaker: BASTARD
text: And spleen of speed to see your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: O cousin, thou art come to set mine eye.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The tackle of my heart is cracked and burnt,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And all the shrouds wherewith my life should sail

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Are turnèd to one thread, one little hair.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: My heart hath one poor string to stay it by,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Which holds but till thy news be utterèd,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And then all this thou seest is but a clod

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And module of confounded royalty.

speaker: BASTARD
text: The Dauphin is preparing hitherward,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Where God He knows how we shall answer him.

speaker: BASTARD
text: For in a night the best part of my power,

speaker: BASTARD
text: As I upon advantage did remove,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Were in the Washes all unwarily

speaker: BASTARD
text: Devourèd by the unexpected flood.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: You breathe these dead news in as dead an ear.—

speaker: SALISBURY
text: My liege! My lord!—But now a king, now thus.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: Even so must I run on, and even so stop.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: What surety of the world, what hope, what stay,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: When this was now a king and now is clay?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Art thou gone so? I do but stay behind

speaker: BASTARD
text: To do the office for thee of revenge,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And then my soul shall wait on thee to heaven,

speaker: BASTARD
text: As it on Earth hath been thy servant still.—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Now, now, you stars, that move in your right spheres,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Where be your powers? Show now your mended faiths

speaker: BASTARD
text: And instantly return with me again

speaker: BASTARD
text: To push destruction and perpetual shame

speaker: BASTARD
text: Out of the weak door of our fainting land.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Straight let us seek, or straight we shall be sought;

speaker: BASTARD
text: The Dauphin rages at our very heels.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: It seems you know not, then, so much as we.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The Cardinal Pandulph is within at rest,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Who half an hour since came from the Dauphin,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And brings from him such offers of our peace

speaker: SALISBURY
text: As we with honor and respect may take,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: With purpose presently to leave this war.

speaker: BASTARD
text: He will the rather do it when he sees

speaker: BASTARD
text: Ourselves well-sinewèd to our defense.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Nay, ’tis in a manner done already,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: For many carriages he hath dispatched

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To the sea-side, and put his cause and quarrel

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To the disposing of the Cardinal,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: With whom yourself, myself, and other lords,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: If you think meet, this afternoon will post

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To consummate this business happily.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Let it be so.—And you, my noble prince,

speaker: BASTARD
text: With other princes that may best be spared,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Shall wait upon your father’s funeral.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: At Worcester must his body be interred,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: For so he willed it.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Thither shall it, then,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And happily may your sweet self put on

speaker: BASTARD
text: The lineal state and glory of the land,

speaker: BASTARD
text: To whom with all submission on my knee

speaker: BASTARD
text: I do bequeath my faithful services

speaker: BASTARD
text: And true subjection everlastingly.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And the like tender of our love we make

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To rest without a spot forevermore.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: I have a kind soul that would give you thanks

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              HENRY
text: And knows not how to do it but with tears.

speaker: BASTARD
text: O, let us pay the time but needful woe,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Since it hath been beforehand with our griefs.

speaker: BASTARD
text: This England never did nor never shall

speaker: BASTARD
text: Lie at the proud foot of a conqueror

speaker: BASTARD
text: But when it first did help to wound itself.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Now these her princes are come home again,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Come the three corners of the world in arms

speaker: BASTARD
text: And we shall shock them. Naught shall make us rue,

speaker: BASTARD
text: If England to itself do rest but true.
